title: The Man Born Blind
subtitle: At the temple gate, the Light of the world anoints blind eyes with clay

stillness: Be still at the threshold of the temple.
To keep the Sabbath spiritually is to be without sin.
Let the noise of the crowd fall away, and wait.
gospel: And as Jesus passed by, going out of the temple,
he saw a man blind from his birth,
who sat there begging alms from those who entered.
Rabbi, who has sinned, this man or his parents,
that he should be born blind?
Jesus answered: Neither has this man sinned, nor his parents;
but that the works of God should be made manifest in him.
He spat upon the ground, and made clay of the spittle,
and spread the clay upon his eyes,
and said to him: Go, wash in the pool of Siloe,
He went therefore, and washed, and he came seeing.
Now it was the Sabbath when these things were done.
And there was a division among them.
behold: Behold attentively, as if you were present.
See how, going out of the temple, the Lord came eagerly to the work that would reveal him.
It was he who saw the blind man; the blind man did not come to him.
He gazed upon him intently, as Chrysostom says, as one who willed to do something for him,
that he might show himself merciful to the wretched.
Behold him spit upon the earth, that the healing power might be shown to flow from his very humanity,
for the saliva flows down from the head.
Behold him make clay of the spittle, to show that he is the one who formed the first man out of the slime of the earth.
Behold the clay smeared over the eyes:
so too the man blinded by the delight of sin must apply, as his remedy, the vileness of that sin.
consider: Consider: this man was not born blind for the punishment of sin,
but that, the wondrous work shown forth in his enlightening, the Son of God might be made manifest.
We are all born children of wrath; the child of wrath is justly punished.
It is of divine mercy when anyone is spared; it is of justice when anyone is punished.
The mercies of the Lord are many, that we are not consumed.
He washed, and came seeing — not by the power of clay or water, but by the divine power that gave him light.
It was the Sabbath; and the Pharisees, knowing this, reproached him.
But they erred: works ordained to the magnifying of God may lawfully be done on the Sabbath.
Whoever commits sin is the servant of sin; and this is servile work.
They kept the Sabbath in the flesh, which in the spirit they were violating.
So even today many follow their own traditions more than the commandments of God.
See the blind man made to see: a constant herald of the truth.
Steadfastly and manfully, even against the great ones of the Jews, he defended the part of his Lord.
He was not ashamed of his former blindness, nor did he dread the fury of the crowd.
Such is the nature of truth: by the very things through which it seems to suffer harm, it grows stronger;
it shines through the very things by which it is darkened.
Truth, when it takes up the despised, makes them bright and strong;
but falsehood, even among the strong, shows them feeble.
At last they cursed him, and said: Be thou his disciple.
It was a curse if you weigh the heart, not if you weigh the words.
Such a curse be upon us, and upon our children!
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who gave light to the eyes of the man born blind,
give light, I beseech you, to the eyes of my heart,
that I may not stumble in the darkness, nor ever fall asleep in death.
O God of my life, how vainly my days have been spent,
how fruitlessly the times have slipped away that you gave me to do your will in them — and I did it not!
How many years, how many months, how many days, how many hours have perished with me,
in which I lived before you without fruit!
Grant, O Father to be loved, that this remnant of my time be fruitful and made holy in your grace,
that in the days of eternity it may find a place, and be counted worthy before you. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Domine Jesu Christe, qui illuminasti oculos cæci nati, illumina, quæso, oculos cordis mei, ne in tenebris offendam, vel umquam in morte obdormiam.
latinJewel.english: Lord Jesus Christ, who gave light to the eyes of the man born blind, give light, I beseech you, to the eyes of my heart, that I may not stumble in the darkness, nor ever fall asleep in death.
